records.ety: [F. rhtorique, L. rhetorica, Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/ (sc. <?/<?/<?/), fr. <?/<?/<?/ rhetorical, oratorical, fr. <?/<?/<?/ orator, rhetorician; perhaps akin to E. word; cf. <?/<?/<?/ to say.]

defs: The art of composition; especially, elegant composition in prose.
Oratory; the art of speaking with propriety, elegance, and force.
Hence, artificial eloquence; fine language or declamation without conviction or earnest feeling.
Fig. : The power of persuasion or attraction; that which allures or charms.
quotes: Sweet, silent rhetoric of persuading eyes.
